query: Describe the tea fields of Minami-Yamashiro village in Kyoto. Just the village, not Uji.
reasoning: RULE A applied: wrong tool called (get_japan_heritage instead of get_local_specialty or search_area), but scored on actual data returned. The response is almost entirely browse_advisory / navigation chrome — zero Minami-Yamashiro tea field content delivered. Specificity is 1 (no named assets, no dates, no field descriptions). Per RULE B, single root cause (retrieval failure / wrong tool) drives the score; other dims drop by at most 1 from their otherwise-deserved values. Groundedness and factual_accuracy land at 3 because the advisory correctly identifies Minami-Yamashiro tea records exist under get_local_specialty. Constraint ('Just the village, not Uji') is entirely unaddressed since no content was retrieved.
missing_data: Any actual content about Minami-Yamashiro (南山城村) tea fields: named tea gardens, cultivar info (e.g. Okumidori, Gokou), harvest seasons, visitor access, scenic viewpoints, production volume, GI/specialty records, or local tea producers.
improvement_hint: The browse_advisory correctly points to get_local_specialty(prefecture='Kyoto') and search_area(q='茶畑') for tea-field queries. The agent (or server intent-routing) should have called one of those instead. If the server can detect a tea-field + specific-municipality intent at routing time, it should auto-redirect or call get_local_specialty with a municipality filter before returning the heritage advisory.
